terms.definition: D_KL(P || Q) = Σ p(x) log(p(x)/q(x)); measures expected log-likelihood ratio of P versus Q when X ~ P. Canonical definition in this topic.
Full name for KL divergence; same quantity D_KL(P || Q), named after Solomon Kullback and Richard Leibler.
Synonym for KL divergence: the extra entropy (or expected code length) incurred by using Q instead of true distribution P.
Shorthand for D_KL(P || Q) with P as reference; mode-seeking behavior in approximate inference when Q is fit to P.
Shorthand for D_KL(Q || P); mean-seeking behavior — Q penalized for covering regions where P has little mass.
